title: gynura malasica
intro: Gynura malasica, also known as "daun gynura" or "purple passion", is a type of medicinal plant native to Southeast Asia, particularly in Malaysia and Indonesia. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Gynura malasica include its potential to reduce fever, alleviate symptoms of diarrhea and dysentery, and even lower cholesterol levels. Gynura malasica is often prepared as a herbal tea or infused in hot water to release its medicinal properties, and it is also used in traditional remedies as a poultice or applied topically to the skin.
